name: Customers Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured bank offering commercial lending, business banking, and personal banking services with $24.8B in total assets and emphasis on personalized service.
description_long: Customers Bank is a Pennsylvania-based FDIC-insured commercial and personal bank founded with a focus on strength and stability since 2009. The bank is headquartered in Malvern, PA and operates with $24.8 billion in total assets, $16.7 billion in total loans and leases, and $11.5 billion in total liquidity as of December 31, 2025. The institution positions itself as a relationship-driven bank that emphasizes knowing customers by name and understanding their industries. The bank offers a comprehensive range of products including commercial lending solutions designed for businesses at various growth stages, personal banking services for employees and communities, and digital banking platforms. They provide business banking support through dedicated phone lines (833-595-0618) and personal banking services (866-476-2265) available 7 days a week from 8AM-11PM ET. Their commercial lending products are specifically designed for entrepreneurs and businesses seeking to expand or start new ventures. Customers Bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on exceptional customer experience and personalized service, with bankers described as empowered to create "Wow" moments. The bank highlights its financial strength through low non-performing assets ratio of 0.29%, substantial liquidity reserves, and consistent focus on stability. They actively publish earnings reports and maintain transparency about their financial position, with Q4'25 earnings webcast available for review. The bank operates as a traditional full-service bank with physical locations and digital capabilities, serving primarily business and commercial clients alongside personal banking customers. While they offer solid financial fundamentals and relationship banking, they are positioned as a regional commercial bank rather than a consumer-focused digital bank, which may limit appeal to customers seeking modern fintech features or specialized consumer lending products.
services: Personal banking for employees and families
Loan and lease products ($16.7 billion portfolio)
pros: FDIC-insured with $24.8 billion in total assets and strong financial position
Low 0.29% non-performing assets ratio indicating quality loan portfolio
$11.5 billion in total liquidity providing financial stability
Extended customer service hours: 7 days a week, 8AM-11PM ET for personal banking
Dedicated business banking line with specialized support (833-595-0618)
Emphasis on personalized relationship banking with industry expertise
Physical branch network with corporate headquarters in Malvern, PA
cons: Limited information about specific interest rates, fees, or APRs on products
Website lacks details about minimum deposit requirements or account specifics
No mention of mobile app features, online banking capabilities, or digital-first services compared to modern neobanks
Primarily positioned toward commercial/business customers rather than individual consumers
Minimal information about credit products, credit cards, or consumer lending options
best_for: Small to mid-sized business owners seeking relationship-based commercial banking
Entrepreneurs needing commercial lending and business expansion financing
Companies valuing personalized service and industry-specific banking expertise
Businesses in Pennsylvania and surrounding regions with physical branch access needs
diagnosis: Customers Bank is best suited for business owners and entrepreneurs seeking personalized commercial banking relationships with a stable, FDIC-insured institution. The main caveat is that this is a traditional regional bank focused primarily on commercial/business clients, not a consumer-focused bank, and their website provides limited transparency about specific products, rates, and consumer lending options.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
